title: Li Auto Weaker Earnings and 2026 Cautious Outlook
text: Li Auto's investment story has been significantly changed by weaker earnings and a cautious 2026 outlook. The company's latest quarterly results revealed earnings challenges that have reshaped investor perspectives on the company's near-term trajectory. In the third quarter, Li Auto missed earnings expectations badly, posting 0.36 yuan per share versus the 0.64 yuan consensus—a 44% shortfall. The latest Q4 2025 results and Q1 2026 guidance, showing year over year revenue and volume declines, make the upcoming L9 and i9 launches the key short term catalyst. Sustained price competition and weak profitability remain the most immediate risk. The company faces challenges in its luxury segment as the AITO M9 has successfully captured a significant portion of Li Auto's L9 customer base, leveraging Huawei's superior brand ecosystem and software integration. BYD's aggressive push into the mid-to-high-end segment with its Fang Cheng Bao and Yangwang brands is squeezing Li Auto's margins. The investment thesis has shifted from growth and profitability to execution risk on new launches and maintaining market share.
